metadata.method_name: Powered self-consistency CLUTRR realized-FDR calibration diagonal for a label-free decoy-competition (knockoff+) FDR gate at the LLM text->logic fact-admission boundary
metadata.description: The single primary artifact is a per-family (atomic / multi_hop) realized-FDR-vs-target-alpha calibration diagonal for the counterfactual-decoy knockoff+ gate, scored under the diagnostic-VALIDATED K=5 self-consistency elicitation on a SCALED, error-dense CLUTRR crisp-gold corpus. Each diagonal row surfaces the (target alpha, decoy_fdr_hat, realized FDR) TRIPLE with a pre-registered self-report disconfirmation, doc-block bootstrap CIs, the swap-decoy negative control, and the plain raw-confidence baseline. Verbalized confidence is run on the SAME data as a documented discreteness/loose-target contrast. An S1b difficulty ladder (L0..L4) repairs-or-bounds the win-rate diagnostic; deterministic foreign-entity entrapment corroborates at alpha*; Generator!=Scorer is carried forward as settled.
metadata.elicitation_selection_rationale: Self-consistency hosts the headline because its counterfactual tail win-rate covers 0.5 (iter-2: 0.482, CI[0.42,0.55]) — the diagnostic-VALIDATED exchangeable regime — whereas single-call VERBALIZED and single-token LOGPROB confidence were flagged anti-conservative in iter-2 (logprob bridge tail win-rate 0.34, CI[0.32,0.37], KS p<1e-20; verbalized overconfident). Note tail-exchangeability, NOT full-distribution AUC, is the selection criterion (iter-2 verbalized full-AUC 0.861 ~ DINCO 0.871, yet verbalized fails the tail test): higher AUC does not imply tail-exchangeability. logprob is NOT re-scored at scale (its iter-2 anti-conservativity is cited); DINCO is not re-run (criterion documented).

hyperparameters.knockoff_plus: Barber-Candes eq 1.9 (the +1 kept; exact FDR control)
hyperparameters.rank_normalization: per-document over {reals U cf(L4) U swap(L1)}; ladder/entrapment items normalized within {pool U that-set} so they share the scale
hyperparameters.alpha_star_definition: smallest CERTIFIED alpha on the populable multi_hop family (n_admitted>=k_floor AND family false-populability>=40); the tightest target the gate certifies => the STRONGEST disconfirmation test

extraction_quality.note: atomic P/R from free extraction; multi-hop accuracy from forced per-pair relation prediction (CLUTRR query format)

paired_exchangeability.interpretation: win-rate ~0.5 (CI covers 0.5) => paired-exchangeable knockoff null holds; < 0.5 => false reals beat their own counterfactual decoys (anti-conservative)

s1b_difficulty_ladder.reason: POWERED rungs show graded/partial sensitivity (detected: ['L2_random_vocab', 'L3_cf_2nd', 'L4_cf_1st']; cover-0.5: ['L1_random_swap']). Under-floor (uninterpreted): none.
s1b_difficulty_ladder.cut_rule: knockoff+ operative T at alpha=0.5 per rung; win-rate over FALSE-real pairs

crux_full_and_tail_self_consistency.decision_relevance_justification: Only the ADMISSION TAIL is decision-relevant: the gate acts solely on pairs with W>=T (the upper tail), so the tail fail-to-reject (decoy ~ spontaneous error) is the operative validity condition. The full-distribution test is reported for completeness; a full-distribution rejection driven by the LOW tail (where the gate never admits) does NOT invalidate tail calibration.

crux_full_and_tail_verbalized.decision_relevance_justification: Only the ADMISSION TAIL is decision-relevant: the gate acts solely on pairs with W>=T (the upper tail), so the tail fail-to-reject (decoy ~ spontaneous error) is the operative validity condition. The full-distribution test is reported for completeness; a full-distribution rejection driven by the LOW tail (where the gate never admits) does NOT invalidate tail calibration.

generator_ne_scorer_carried_forward.validity_region_statement: Decoy-competition exchangeability (tail win-rate CI covers 0.5) holds for configs: G=nano,S=nano, G=nano,S=other, G=other,S=nano, G=other,S=other. Verdict: ROBUST.
generator_ne_scorer_carried_forward.note: SETTLED in iter-2 (4/4 configs cover 0.5 incl. cross-family ministral-8b); embedded as provenance, NO new budget spent.

paired_vs_marginal.explanation: The MARGINAL crux (crux_full_and_tail_*) tests whether the counterfactual-decoy score DISTRIBUTION matches the spontaneous-error distribution (a population test). The PAIRED statistic (each diagonal row's paired_exchangeability AND paired_across_GS) tests, per real, whether the false real beats its OWN decoy — the actual knockoff+ sign-flip the gate's FDR theorem requires. They can diverge: the marginal can hold while the paired fails (or vice versa). Both are reported DISTINCTLY so the validity argument is evidenced, not asserted.

multi_hop_paired_exchangeability.interpretation: win-rate ~0.5 (CI covers 0.5) => paired-exchangeable knockoff null holds; < 0.5 => false reals beat their own counterfactual decoys (anti-conservative)

atomic_paired_exchangeability.interpretation: win-rate ~0.5 (CI covers 0.5) => paired-exchangeable knockoff null holds; < 0.5 => false reals beat their own counterfactual decoys (anti-conservative)

paired_across_GS.interpretation: No (G,S) config reached the 20-false-pair floor on the 0 warm docs; the paired (G,S) de-circularization is UNDERPOWERED (reported, not interpreted). The MARGINAL G!=S robustness is carried from iter-2.
paired_across_GS.marginal_gs_carried: Generator!=Scorer MARGINAL exchangeability settled in iter-2 (4/4 configs' tail win-rate CI cover 0.5 incl. ministral-8b).

density_strata.interpretation: The realized-FDR breach PERSISTS across density strata (including the lowest powered density): the failure is NOT merely error-density-driven.
density_strata.note: FREE (0 API) k-density de-confound. The FULL extractor-strength x false-positive-density matrix is owned by the sibling iter-4 artifact (dir2); the optional strong-extractor preview here is metadata.strong_extractor_arm.

strong_extractor_arm.reason: strong-extractor arm not run (budget-gated off or --strong not set). The FULL extractor-strength x false-positive-density matrix is delegated to the sibling iter-4 artifact (dir2); the FREE k-density stratification (metadata.density_strata) is P1's self-contained de-confound.

primary_disconfirmation_verdict.reason: realized FDR 1.0 > alpha*+tau (0.5+0.05) AND doc-block CI [0.661594,1.0] lies entirely above alpha*=0.5.

paired_exchangeability.interpretation: win-rate ~0.5 (CI covers 0.5) => paired-exchangeable knockoff null holds; < 0.5 => false reals beat their own counterfactual decoys (anti-conservative)

primary_disconfirmation_verdict.mechanism_note: Reconcile the verdict with the crux/paired-exchangeability diagnostic: if the cf tail win-rate over FALSE pairs covers 0.5 the knockoff null holds and any realized-FDR breach is a true/false NON-SEPARATION effect (confidence fails to rank true above false on this family); if win-rate < 0.5 the false reals beat their own counterfactual decoys (decoys too easy under self-consistency).

metadata.reconciliation_narrative: ONE consolidated diagonal story: under the validated self-consistency elicitation the per-family knockoff+ diagonal is the single primary calibration result; the verbalized diagonal on the SAME data is a wrong-elicitation discreteness/loose-target ARTIFACT (see contrast_diagonal_verbalized.artifact_notes), not a co-headline. This reconciles iter-2: self-consistency exchangeable (cf tail win-rate ~0.48), verbalized/logprob anti-conservative.

interpretation.diagonal_expected: realized FDR <= target alpha (+tau) at certified alphas under self-consistency; the knockoff+ gate is conservative.
interpretation.self_report_check: decoy_fdr_hat is DISCONFIRMED at any alpha where it is anti-conservative vs realized beyond tau (even if realized < alpha).
interpretation.ladder_expected: easy rungs (L0 foreign-swap, L1 in-doc swap) flagged anti-conservative while hard L4-cf covers 0.5 => REPAIRED; if even L0 covers 0.5 => BLIND_LIMITATION (diagnostic down-scoped).
interpretation.crux_expected: decoy ~ spontaneous-error in the admission tail (fail-to-reject) and != true-positive (reject); full-distribution result reported with tail-only decision-relevance justification.
interpretation.entrapment_expected: combined FDP ~ realized at alpha*; divergence at alpha=0.5 reported.
